title: [Feature]: Add optional WebSocket connection header support
problem: Some WebSocket deployments require custom headers for proxy routing or controlled authentication layers. nats-py supports ws_connection_headers, but exposing arbitrary headers in nats-sinks configuration could leak secrets or make logs unsafe.
proposal: Add an optional WebSocket header configuration model that is disabled by default, validates header names and bounded values, supports environment-variable references for sensitive values, rejects dangerous or ambiguous headers, redacts all values in effective configuration, and passes approved headers to nats-py as ws_connection_headers.
users: Operators behind approved WebSocket gateways or reverse proxies and security teams reviewing controlled header-based routing requirements.
delivery_semantics: WebSocket headers affect only connection establishment. They must not affect sink writes, ACK behavior, DLQ behavior, retry decisions, or idempotency.
security: Never log header values. Reject multiline values and control characters. Do not support user-provided arbitrary Authorization header values unless they come from an explicit environment-variable source and are redacted everywhere. Document proxy-log exposure risks.
acceptance: Header support is disabled unless explicitly configured.
Header names and values are validated and size bounded.
Sensitive header values can be sourced from environment variables without appearing in JSON examples.
Redacted effective config never prints header values.
nats-py receives headers only after validation succeeds.
tests: Add unit tests for valid headers, invalid names, control characters, oversized values, environment-variable resolution, redaction, and nats-py option construction. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update the WebSocket evaluation, configuration, security, operations, and NATS connection documentation with safe examples and clear warnings.
closeout: Close after the release containing optional WebSocket header support is published. Include tests, documentation, and limitations.
